title: BASE vs SaaS login isolation hardening
description:
Full audit of provisioning and traffic control confirmed login walls on POST /login, but authenticated API calls did not re-check environment — a SaaS session token could still work on pipeshare.live until it expired.

requireAuth now enforces the same BASE vs SaaS rules on every request. Hosting tier labels treat tenant-bound portal scopes as SAAS (fixes employees mislabeled BASE). Sign-up on pipeshare.live rejects SaaS company provisioning. Login and portal session resume clear tokens on environment 403 with the server redirect message.

Who is affected: everyone using PipeShare BASE (pipeshare.live / private server) and PipeShare SaaS (pipeshare.net / tenant subdomains). SaaS tenants receive this when the next platform version is applied in cPanel releases.
changeLog: Block session reuse across BASE and SaaS on every authenticated API call.
Classify tenant-* portal scopes as SAAS hosting tier (not BASE employees).
Reject SaaS company sign-up attempts on the base PipeShare server.
Clear stale sessions on environment mismatch during login resume and portal validation.
